reason: Project boundary, source-of-truth order, RVV-first authority chain, and Stage1/Stage2 sequencing constraints.
RVV plugin-owned route support, typed body/config authority, elementwise coverage, provider construction, artifact ABI consumer, fail-closed diagnostics, and mirror metadata rules.
tcrv.exec envelope and ABI/runtime binding boundary; compute and RVV semantics must stay outside core exec.
TCRVEmitCLowerableRoute and common EmitC neutrality contract for provider-derived route facts.
Required lit/C++/runtime evidence types and RVV generated artifact evidence quality rules.

summary: Implemented typed elementwise route-family derivation snapshot in RVV route planning/provider. The elementwise plan now carries element type, signed C type, SEW, LMUL, tail/mask policy, and config contract from typed tcrv_rvv body/config facts; provider construction verifies materialization facts against that plan before building TCRVEmitCLowerableRoute.

Strengthened RVV target artifact consumer validation for elementwise arithmetic routes. The target boundary now checks provider-supported mirror labels, route family facts, runtime ABI order/roles, runtime n/AVL loop structure, signed element C types, required headers, type mappings, selected capability mirrors, mask/strided stale metadata rejection, and common EmitC neutrality before emitting artifacts.

Expanded lmul_m2_add witness tests to match i64_add evidence depth: dry-run evidence now checks provider-derived element type, signed C type, SEW, LMUL, vector C type, e32m2 intrinsic family, runtime AVL/VL facts, explicit mirror-only metadata, and generated C++ intrinsics. Target artifact checks now include elementwise route-family plan, runtime control, headers, C type mapping, and operand binding mirrors.
